sourceMovie.title: Unbroken
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Unbroken (2014) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Angelina Jolie's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and war blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Lone Survivor
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Unbroken clicked for you, Lone Survivor (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and war, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Unbroken?
faq.answer: Try Lone Survivor, The Thin Red Line, The Great Escape—each shares DNA with Unbroken while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Unbroken?
faq.answer: Unbroken (2014) is best described as Drama, War. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Unbroken part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Unbroken Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
